results.explanation: New rule prescribes tapping at ≥1620°C minimum; candidate forbids tapping above 1610°C. Same metric and conditions, directly opposing thresholds with no overlap.
results.shared_conditions: Tap temperature decision on standard carbon-steel grades in EAF process.

results.explanation: New statement claims late aluminium addition raises tap temperature by 8-12°C; candidate A-EAF-003 claims it lowers tap temperature by 5-10°C. Same scope (low-Si aim chemistry), same phenomenon, directly opposite factual claims.
results.shared_conditions: Late aluminium addition timing on low-Si aim chemistry heats in EAF process.

results.explanation: New rule injects carbon after flat bath is established (at >28% FeO); candidate explicitly stops carbon injection once flat bath is established. Same condition (flat bath reached), opposing actions.
results.shared_conditions: Carbon injection timing once flat bath is established during EAF heat cycle.

results.explanation: New rule prescribes increasing casting speed when oscillation exceeds 5 mm; candidate prescribes reducing speed by 0.2 m/min under identical conditions. Same threshold, same equipment and grade, directly opposing actions.
results.shared_conditions: Mould level oscillation exceeding 5 mm on slab caster for peritectic grades.

results.explanation: New rule permits tapping at 0.020% P; candidate forbids tapping above 0.015% P. Same metric, same grades/route, directly opposing thresholds.
results.shared_conditions: S355, A572 structural grades, EAF route, phosphorus control at tap.

results.explanation: New rule prescribes FeMn before FeSi; candidate prescribes FeSi before FeMn. Both apply to the same process (deoxidation sequence for Si and Mn alloying) under the same conditions, with directly opposite action order.
results.shared_conditions: All heats requiring silicon and manganese alloying during deoxidation in EAF process.

results.explanation: New rule prescribes light scrap on top of heavy scrap; candidate prescribes heavy scrap on top of light scrap. Same bucket, same charging step, directly opposing scrap layer order.
results.shared_conditions: Two-bucket charging system, first bucket load sequence, MD1 EAF plant.

results.explanation: New rule says continue casting when SEN drive current rises 5% above baseline; candidate R-EAF-018 says intervene (swap SEN) at the same 5% threshold. Same metric, same condition, opposite prescribed actions.
results.shared_conditions: SEN drive current at 5% above baseline threshold, all slab grades, EAF continuous casting process.

results.explanation: New rule prescribes 1180–1210°C reheat; candidate prescribes 1230–1260°C. Same process (slab reheat before roughing), same grades, but incompatible temperature targets. Candidate rationale (stiffness/motor overload below 1230°C) directly co
results.shared_conditions: Slab reheat temperature before roughing stand entry on structural grades in MD1 EAF Plant.

results.explanation: New rule accepts sealed vessels if visually cut open; candidate rules out sealed vessels with zero tolerance. Same condition (sealed containers in incoming scrap), opposite actions.
results.shared_conditions: All incoming scrap deliveries, sealed pressure vessels or containers in charge material, EAF meltdown process.

results.explanation: New statement requires certificate verification within 2 hours of delivery (traceability/documentation); candidate requires physical scrap inspection for moisture/sealed containers (safety). Both apply to incoming scrap but address differen
New statement specifies the generic 'bad scrap' rule with a concrete metric (Sims >50%) and quantified action (15% speed reduction) on a defined equipment scope (slab caster, peritectic grades).
results.shared_conditions: Casting speed reduction in response to elevated residual copper from poor scrap quality on the slab caster.

results.explanation: New statement specifies a longer preheat (90+15=105 min) for fresh lining after changeout, refining the baseline 90-minute rule which applies to established linings. Same equipment and process, different lining age condition.
results.shared_conditions: Tundish preheat duration before casting, EAF process area, MD1 plant.

results.explanation: New rule applies during Ca-wire injection phase (reduce stirring to soft bubble); candidate applies pre-sample stirring phase (maintain 80 Nl/min minimum). Different process stages with different goals—no conflict.

New statement addresses roll surface sampling and wear detection in finishing stands; candidate addresses moisture removal from chemistry samples before spectrometer analysis. Different equipment, different processes, no overlap.

New statement specifies mould oscillation for casting speeds above 1.6 m/min (220 strokes/min), while candidate covers 1.2–1.6 m/min (180 strokes/min). They agree on the principle and target (negative strip time), but new rule extends into
results.shared_conditions: Slab caster mould oscillation control to maintain negative strip time across the casting speed operating window.

results.explanation: New statement addresses electrode consumption in EAF melting; candidate addresses coiler tension in hot strip mill. Different process areas and equipment.

New statement addresses post-alloy soft-bubble rinse and inclusion flotation; candidate addresses gas selection (argon vs nitrogen) for nitrogen control in low-N grades. Different process stages and metallurgical objectives.

New statement specifies the numeric threshold (15 kN) and detection method (load cells) for the abnormal friction condition that candidate R-EAF-020 describes generically. Both prescribe stopping casting on mould friction issues; the new ru
results.shared_conditions: Mould friction monitoring during casting on all grades in EAF plant operations.

results.explanation: Near-duplicate of R-EAF-028: identical temperature target (1230–1260°C), tolerance (±15°C), scope (structural and commercial grades), and rationale. New statement adds no new information or refinement.
